title: fagraea dasyantha
intro: Fagraea dasyantha, also known as the fragrant frangipani, is a flowering plant species in the family Loganiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. The plant is known for its fragrant white flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Fagraea dasyantha include its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. Fagraea dasyantha is often prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as a tincture or decoction, for its medicinal uses.
